title: Doctor in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **For real emergencies in Berlin, call 112.** Use it for severe symptoms, major accidents, breathing trouble, heavy bleeding, collapse or anything unsafe to wait on.
**For urgent but non-life-threatening medical help, use 116117.** It is the German medical on-call route for problems that cannot comfortably wait until the next working day.
**For stable problems, a normal doctor is usually calmer than a hospital emergency department.** Search for Allgemeinmedizin, Hausarzt, Praxis or MVZ near your hotel.
**For medicine questions, start with an Apotheke.** A pharmacy can help with over-the-counter advice, prescription handling and whether a doctor route is needed.
**Bring insurance details and keep receipts.** EHIC helps EU visitors with medically necessary public care, but travel insurance still matters for private treatment and claims.
